B-llvm-bugsuppression-2025.relevance-description: Shows Clang's explicit-suppression model: attribute on a decl creates a SourceRange; a bug is suppressed iff its location is fullyContained. Range-containment suppression is a candidate model for Dorc's per-site suppression, but this is user-driven not cause-driven; the cascade-dedup we need is in BugReporterVisitors/BugReporter.

A-llvm-bugreportervisitors-2025.relevance-description: THE primary source for rq-E. Shows (1) whole-report heuristic suppression via markInvalid on a known-FP denylist; (2) the interestingness-gated note pattern: notes emit text only if their subject symbol isInteresting() to the report, else empty string => self-suppress. This is Clang's direct analog of Dorc's '⊤ carries a cause-pointer; only cause-site notes are interesting'.

A-llvm-bugreporter-2025.relevance-description: THE primary source for rq-E dedup. removeRedundantMsgs+eventsDescribeSameCondition = two-visitors-same-location -> keep-one-by-preference (Dorc's per-⊤-operand multiplication shape). removeUnneededCalls = recursive interestingness-gated pruning of whole subpaths. markInteresting(StackFrame) propagates a cause-chain backward; events default isPrunable()=true. This is the mechanized version of 'only root-cause must be reported'.
